signals.reasoning: This is still a protect-the-position call. The live BLUR long has only retained about 43% of peak P&L, the request flags it urgent, and the active veto is the wrong environment to keep hoping for a recovery. I would close it and protect what is left.
BTC is stable and liquid, but the board still marks it no-trade and the active veto argues against opening fresh risk. Neutral is still the right stance.
ETH is mildly constructive but still low-edge, with positive funding and no-trade status on the board. Not enough asymmetry to act on under veto conditions.
SOL has good intraday strength, but funding is positive and the board remains no-trade. It is stronger than ETH, but still not a clean entry during a veto regime.
DASH has strong momentum and decent volume, but the board is still neutral and funding is positive. Interesting watchlist name, not a live trade.
MON has become momentum-positive and no longer offers a clean bearish asymmetry. It does not stand out enough on either side here.
PYTH has one of the better secondary long profiles with positive trend and slightly supportive funding, but it is still not strong enough to override the active veto.
DYM is constructive but no longer has the strong negative-funding squeeze profile that made it attractive earlier. Under current conditions it drops to watch-only.
ARB has decent liquidity and a mild bullish lean, but not enough edge to matter through a live geopolitical veto.
ZEN has respectable strength, but funding is positive and the board remains neutral. Watchlist only.
TNSR still screens as a valid squeeze-continuation long mechanically, but the active veto is a direct reason not to open fresh high-beta exposure. Strong watchlist long, not a live trade.
